### Post by twdinger on 2006-11-19
Hello,

I have been trying to setup dual monitors for about 8 hours now and I cannot get this figured out. Here is my Xorg.conf file:

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)

FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"

Load "dri"

Load "int10"

Option "CoreKeyboard"

Option "XkbModel" "pc105"
Option "XkbLayout" "us"
Option "XkbVariant" "intl"

Screen 1

Modes "1024x768" "800x856" "800x600" "640x480"

EndSubSection

Depth 8
Modes "1024x768" "800x856" "800x600" "640x480"
EndSubSection
SubSection "Display"
Depth 15
Modes "1024x768" "800x856" "800x600" "640x480"
EndSubSection

Depth 16
Modes "1024x768" "800x856" "800x600" "640x480"
EndSubSection
SubSection "Display"
Depth 24
Modes "1024x768" "800x856" "800x600" "640x480"
EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"

EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
Identifier "Dual Monitor Layout"
Screen 0 "LeftScreen" 0 0
Screen 1 "RightScreen" RightOf "LeftScreen"
# Option "Xinerama" "on"

InputDevice "Configured Mouse"

InputDevice "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
InputDevice "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"

Section "DRI"
Mode 0666
EndSection

_______________

I get screen 0 to work just fine, but screen 1 is black with a blinking cursor in the top left hand corner. I have verified that both monitors work by switching screen 1 to screen 0 and vise versa in the "Device" section. At first I wasn't using Xinerama with the same outcome, black screen white blinking cursor. So, I tried enabling Xinerama in the "ServerLayout" section with same luck. Then I commented that line out and tried it as a "ServerFlag", same thing. I tried using "on" and "true" in both sections with no luck. The VIA driver is onboard video and the ATI driver is a PCI card. I do have dual monitors working in Windows XP with the same machine, so I know the hardware is all working. Can anyone see what I am doing wrong?

### Post by krismatth3 on 2006-11-19
Try removing "Screen 0" and "Screen 1" from the device declarations:

```

Section "Device"
Identifier "VIA Technologies, Inc. S3 Unichrome Pro VGA Adapter"
Driver "via"
BusID "PCI:1:0:0"
Screen 0
EndSection

Section "Device"
Identifier "ATI Technologies, Inc. 3D Rage Pro 215GP"
Driver "ati"
BusID "PCI:0:8:0"
Screen 1
EndSection

```

"Screen" in that context is only meant for devices that have more than one output option; as you're using two seperate cards, it is unnecessary.

---

### Post by krismatth3 on 2006-11-19
Here's my xorg.conf. Notice that I am only using "Screen 0/1" on the card that has two outputs - the second card (for the third monitor) does not have any "Screen 0/1" entry.

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
	Option		"XkbOptions"	"lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"CenterCard"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:3:0:0"
	Screen		0
	Option		NoLogo
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"RightCard"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:3:0:0"
	Screen		1
	Option		NoLogo
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"LeftCard"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:4:7:0"
	Option		NoLogo
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"CenterMonitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"RightMonitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30.0 - 81.0
	VertRefresh	56.0 - 75.0
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"LeftMonitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"CenterScreen"
	Device		"CenterCard"
	Monitor		"CenterMonitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"RightScreen"
	Device		"RightCard"
	Monitor		"RightMonitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"LeftScreen"
	Device		"LeftCard"
	Monitor		"LeftMonitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0 "CenterScreen"
	Screen		1 "RightScreen" RightOf "CenterScreen"
	Screen		2 "LeftScreen" LeftOf "CenterScreen"
	Option		"Xinerama" "on"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option "Composite" "false"
EndSection

```

### Post by CatKiller on 2006-11-19
Some random thoughts:

krism is right - take out the Screen 0 and Screen 1 lines from the Device sections. "Screen 1" is meaningless in your configuration.

You could try setting your motherboard's BIOS to start the PCI card first. I know my dual screen setup didn't work until I did that (in Windows or Ubuntu). And even with it all properly configured, sometimes the wind is in the wrong direction and the second card doesn't initialise. Restarting X fixes it though.

You could also try putting in appropriate values for HorizSync and VertRefresh for the monitor that doesn't have them. Also, [this page]("http://support.gateway.com/s/MONITOR/7003421/700342102.shtml") suggests that the timings that you **do** have are wrong, and that they should be ```
        HorizSync       31-69
        VertRefresh     50-160
 
```

The X log is at /var/log/Xorg.0.log if you want to look for messages about what's wrong.

---

### Post by twdinger on 2006-11-19
I have already tried changing the BIOS, that was probably the first thing I tried.  As for the HorizSync and VertRefresh values, I got those from using a live cd, I didn't expect them to work, but you never know.  But like I said, if I switch the 0 and the 1, the second monitor works displaying the Desktop and the first monitor stops working and gives me the cursor.  So i don't think the HorizSync and VertRefresh values are affecting it too much.  I will try commenting out Screen 1.

Thanks

You don't think there would be a conflict between video cards do you?
